title: centaurea collina
intro: Centaurea collina, also known as mountain cornflower, is a perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of the Balkan Peninsula and the Mediterranean. It has been used in traditional medicine due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it effective in treating various health issues. The plant's medicinal properties are believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating digestive problems, and acting as an antiseptic to promote wound healing. Centaurea collina can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures for its medicinal purposes, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and optimal usage.
